structuralBlockers: Requires willingness to relocate to Austin/Fremont; remote not offered and not addressable by study.
Demands production experience shipping low-latency participant-facing systems; self-taught frontend/ML-prototyping background does not substitute.
Must translate primary neuroscience papers directly into clinical code under weekly participant sessions; no domain track record exists.

alternativeTargetBranches.role: Software Engineer, Real-time Systems at robotics or embedded hardware company
alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Matches low-latency systems focus and production ownership without requiring neuroscience domain or clinical constraints.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Lower total comp than Neuralink; narrower scope on device control but faster path to relevant experience.

alternativeTargetBranches.role: ML Engineer, Decoding Systems at non-clinical neurotech or research lab
alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Leverages Python/ML prototyping strength and paper-to-code skill; avoids full clinical/regulatory load.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Fewer participant-facing UX responsibilities; reduced ownership of end-to-end shipping cadence.

alternativeTargetBranches.role: Full-stack Engineer, Healthtech applications at regulated medical device startup
alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Builds on Next.js/TypeScript strength while adding domain exposure in a slower-paced clinical environment.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Lower velocity and technical depth on real-time pipelines; longer timeline to BCI-specific work.

clusters.name: Real-time Low-Latency Systems
